findings.issue: Verify-user step (c) asks to change max_tokens via the Settings bottom-sheet 'slider' and confirm HeavyChangeDialog. Task 3 removes MAX_TOKENS from LIGHT_FIELD_LABELS and reclassifies it to HEAVY in classifyApplyLevel, but the task does not explicitly state that the bottom-sheet UI continues to render a max_tokens control (the slider element rendering lives outside Task 3 scope). If the sheet renders fields by iterating LIGHT_FIELD_LABELS or a similar set, removing MAX_TOKENS from it could hide the control entirely, which would make the user-step impossible to execute.
findings.fix: Add a one-line note in Task 3 § Details / Implementation hints clarifying that the max_tokens control in the bottom-sheet stays rendered (it is fed by the global ConfigKeys list, not by LIGHT_FIELD_LABELS — the latter only drives Light/Heavy classification). If verification cannot rely on this, the user-step should reference adb / direct DataStore edit instead of the sheet.

findings.issue: AC-3.3 visual smoke includes 'Crash Report' as one of the 6 screens, but Task 4 acknowledges in the same step that there is no debug trigger to reach CrashReportActivity reliably ('искусственно вызванный краш или debug-trigger, если есть; иначе — отдельный отчёт'). This leaves an ambiguous user-step: the verifier may not be able to execute it deterministically.
findings.fix: Either add a documented debug-trigger to Task 4's What-to-do (one-line shortcut, e.g. setting that throws on resume), OR explicitly mark the Crash-Report sub-step as deferred/optional in the user-checklist with a follow-up note in Task 10's QA matrix.

findings.issue: Frontmatter declares `skills: [security-auditor]`. The skill folder `~/.claude/skills/security-auditor/` exists and resolves cleanly, so this is not a critical mismatch — but the skill catalog convention for review/audit tasks across this batch is not perfectly uniform (Task 7 uses `code-reviewing`, Task 9 uses `test-master`, Task 8 uses `security-auditor`). Reader risk: easy to confuse the skill name with the agent name of the same string.
findings.fix: No change required if SKILL.md exists at `~/.claude/skills/security-auditor/SKILL.md` (it does). Optionally add a one-line note in § Required Skills clarifying that `security-auditor` here is the SKILL (methodology), distinct from the homonymous reviewer-agent invoked elsewhere.

findings.issue: User-smoke step 5 (AC-1.5 logs export) instructs the user to run `adb logcat -d | grep inference-reset` OR use Diagnostics → Export logs. But ErrorLog writes to a file in app private storage (`logs/errors.log`), not to logcat. If `adb logcat` does not surface ErrorLog entries, the alternative path through Diagnostics export is the only reliable channel — and that channel relies on Phase-2 Diagnostics-screen export functionality being wired to ErrorLog (not verified here).
findings.fix: Drop the `adb logcat` alternative or replace it with `adb pull` of the app's private logs/errors.log via run-as <pkg>. Verify Diagnostics export covers ErrorLog before final QA.

findings.issue: User-smoke for Bug 2 fix is correctly listed under Task 6 (Settings IconButton gating). Task 3's user-step (c) for max_tokens HeavyDialog requires a Persistent chat with Ready engine where Settings is already enabled (pre-Task-6 state covers Persistent fine). This is consistent with the user's R1 input — flagging only as observation, not violation.
findings.fix: No change required. Documented here for traceability.

task_3.refs: drawer (existing), Settings sheet (existing in Persistent chat at Ready), max_tokens slider (existing UI control)

task_6.refs: Settings IconButton in TopAppBar — gating delivered by this task

task_10.refs: QA orchestrator drives full user smoke
